visa_types.overview:
Overview
Business Visa
A Business visa is required if during any portion of the visit customer intends to conduct business or commercial activities. e.g.; Meetings, Conference, Trainings, Trade Fairs, etc.; such visas can only be issued to travelers not being paid for work by an entity within Belgium. If the customer intends to undertake paid work in Belgium, he/she will need to obtain a work permit.        
Note
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Business Visa
A Business visa is required if during any portion of the visit customer intends to conduct business or commercial activities. e.g.; Meetings, Conference, Trainings, Trade Fairs, etc.; such visas can only be issued to travelers not being paid for work by an entity within Belgium. If the customer intends to undertake paid work in Belgium, he/she will need to obtain a work permit.        
Note
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Visa Fees

[bel > vnm > en > Table:Tourist Visa > Visa Fees 1]

The applicable visa fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change. The Belgium Embassy in Vietnam is the only entity which is authorized to change the rates provided. All fees are non-refundable unless instructed by the Embassy. 
Visa fee, VFS service fee of VND 682,000 and all optional value added services fees will be charged in cash or card at VFS counter.
For customers’ convenience, VFS provides additional services to support customers in the process of their visa application with VFS. Please “click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/bel/additional-services)”

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/bel/travel-insurance)
Please click here for Documents required

Note: Applicant needs to submit one set of original documents in A4 paper with NO staples, pins.
For more information about legalization procedure, please visit the Embassy’s link: 
https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents (https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents)
All documents to be legalized will be treated directly by the Embassy. The legalized document (if not yet done at the moment of visa submission) will be added to the file later on.
Note:
Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The customer is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance: proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the customer and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the customer to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards: 
All visa applications must include one recent  photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken        against a plain light background
- Size        35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face        must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral        expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised        eyebrows)
- Eyes        must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair        must not cover face
- No        reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your        eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The        photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have        a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed        professionally
- Eyes        open and clearly visible
- Full        head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs        or medical reasons
- CHILDREN:        photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness        of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s        head, it is important, that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
The handling time of the various applications will depend on the case load. The handling time is calculated from the moment an application has been registered in the visa handling system.
For Schengen visa, the embassy normally makes a decision in the case within a period of 15 calendar days.  That period may be extended up to a maximum of 30 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed. Exceptionally, when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.

## Download Forms
Visa Application Short – Stay Form (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/)
All customers are    required to fill the Visa Application form online on the website: https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/) before    submitting documents at VFS. In case the customers visit Belgium Visa    Application Centre without online application form in advance, they can use    VFS Filling form service with fee applied.

visa_types.name: Long - Stay Visa
visa_types.overview:
A visa for “long stay” is applicable for people who remain a long time (more than 90 days) or who establish themselves definitively in Belgium.
Since the 2 March 2015 some foreign nationals have to pay a  contribution which is to cover the administrative costs involved with the processing  by the Immigration Office of a long stay visa application for Belgium. When  submitting the visa application, the customer who is required to pay the  contribution shall have to demonstrate that the contribution has been paid in  full. For additional information, please consult the webpage of the Belgian  Immigration Office at the following address:  Click here (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/faq/fee)
- A visa normally grants you the right to stay in the entire Schengen region. The Schengen countries are: Austria, Belgium, Croatia, the Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, the Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland.
A visa grants you the right to spend more than 90 days in the Schengen region.
All visa applications should be signed by the customer personally. 
- All fields in the application form must be filled in correctly, incomplete forms may cause delay in processing or even rejection.
- The Embassy reserves the right to request additional documents and/or information when processing the application.
In some cases, the following documents are required:
- The extract of the criminal record (please refer "extract of the criminal record" to link:
https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/vi/visa-for-belgium/forms (https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/vi/visa-for-belgium/forms)
)
- The
medical certificate (https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/sites/default/files/content/doctors_list.pdf )
The medical certificates must be obtained in hospitals recognized by the embassy. 
Change  from 01 December 2018: capture of fingerprints  for Long Stay Visa (D) for Belgium
By taking  your fingerprints you can be identified with certainty as the sole holder of  the visa which enhances security for everyone, including the visa customer.  This protects you against forgery or misuse of identity - for example in the  event of theft or loss of your passport – and facilitates checks at external  borders.
Finger  prints for D visa cannot be re used for short stay visa (C Schengen visa)
For more  information please consult the website of Home Affairs FPS Immigration Office (https://dofi.ibz.be/en)
Specifically for Vietnam:
All applications should be lodged at the Belgian Visa Application Center in Hanoi, Da Nang or Ho Chi Minh City . Personal appearance is in principle compulsory.
Categories  of exemption of personal appearance:
- Children under the age of 6 (for D visa) and under the age of 12 (for C visa);
- Persons for whom the collection of fingerprints is physically impossible;
- Heads of State and members of the National Government, with accompanying spouses, and the members of their official delegation when they are invited by Member States’ Governments or by International Governments or by International Organizations for an official purpose.
- Persons who have already submitted fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Visa For Au Pair
Customers applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.

## Visa Fees

[bel > vnm > en > Table:Cohabitation with Belgian or non EU citizen > Visa Fees 1]
As of July 1, 2026, long-stay visa fees will amount to 7.750.000 VND. If your appointment takes place after June 30, 2026, you will be required to pay 7.750.000 VND when submitting your Visa D application. 
The applicable visa fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change. The Belgium Embassy in Vietnam is the only entity which is authorized to change the rates provided. All fees are non-refundable unless instructed by the Embassy. 
Visa fee, VFS service fee of VND 682,000 and all optional value added services fees will be charged in cash or card at VFS counter.
For customers’ convenience, VFS provides additional services to support customers in the process of their visa application with VFS. Please “click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/bel/additional-services)”

## Documents Required
Please click here for Documents required

Note: Applicant needs to submit one set of original documents in A4 paper with NO staples, pins.
For more information about legalization procedure, please visit the Embassy’s link: 
https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents (https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents)
All documents to be legalized will be treated directly by the Embassy or the Consulate. The legalized document (if not yet done at the moment of visa submission) will be added to the file later on.

## New contribution for visa LONG stay - D

Since the 2nd of March 2015, a contribution is required for the processing of certain long stay (D) visa applications.

  This contribution can be paid by either the customer or  a third person (family members, acquaintance, sponsor, guarantor, etc.). This  contribution should be paid into  the account of the Immigration Office in Belgium.

  The proof  of payment of this contribution needs to be  presented at the  time of the introduction of the visa application. Should  the customer be unable to provide a proof of payment, the visa application  will be deemed inadmissible.

  This contribution is to be paid on top of the handling  fee of the long stay visa (handling fee = the equivalent of €180 in local  currency). The handling fee will still need to be paid at the time of the  application. An exemption for the handling fee, does not necessarily imply that  one is also exempt for the contribution.

  
 
 We therefore recommend you to carefully consult the website of the Immigration Office  (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/faq/fee) with regards to the amount of the contribution and the  manner in which this is to be paid.
Attention
- Any bank charges or charges       related to the transfer will need to be borne by the customer or whomever       performs the transfer.
- In the event that you have not       paid the full amount, you will have an additional 30 days to transfer the       additional amount. If you do not provide the proof that the full amount       was paid within this period, the       amount already paid will not be reimbursed and the       application will be inadmissible.
- The contribution will       likewise not       be reimbursed in the event of a refusal of the visa.

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards: 
All visa applications must include one recent  photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken        against a plain light background
- Size        35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face        must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral        expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised        eyebrows)
- Eyes        must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair        must not cover face
- No        reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your        eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The        photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have        a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed        professionally
- Eyes        open and clearly visible
- Full        head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs        or medical reasons
- CHILDREN:        photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness        of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s        head, it is important, that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
Processing Time : 15 Calendar Days after application is consider admissible. In individual cases, where further scrutiny of the application is needed by the Immigration department in Brussels, the deadline may be extended up to a maximum of 6 months after the application has been considered admissible.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Visa for Marriage In Belgium With Belgian Or Non-Eu Citizen
The visa is for Vietnamese and Foreign nationals who intend to get married to Belgian Nationals and Non-EU Nationals in Belgium.
Note
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Visa Fees

[bel > vnm > en > Table:Cohabitation with Belgian or non EU citizen > Visa Fees 1]
As of July 1, 2026, long-stay visa fees will amount to 7.750.000 VND. If your appointment takes place after June 30, 2026, you will be required to pay 7.750.000 VND when submitting your Visa D application. 
The applicable visa fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change. The Belgium Embassy in Vietnam is the only entity which is authorized to change the rates provided. All fees are non-refundable unless instructed by the Embassy. 
Visa fee, VFS service fee of VND 682,000 and all optional value added services fees will be charged in cash or card at VFS counter.
For customers’ convenience, VFS provides additional services to support customers in the process of their visa application with VFS. Please “click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/bel/additional-services)”

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/bel/travel-insurance)

Please click here for Documents required

Note: Applicant needs to submit one set of original documents in A4 paper with NO staples, pins.
For more information about legalization procedure, please visit the Embassy’s link: 
https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents (https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents)
All documents to be legalized will be treated directly by the Embassy. The legalized document (if not yet done at the moment of visa submission) will be added to the file later on.
Note:
Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The customer is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance: proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the customer and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the customer to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards: 
All visa applications must include one recent  photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken        against a plain light background
- Size        35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face        must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral        expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised        eyebrows)
- Eyes        must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair        must not cover face
- No        reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your        eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The        photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have        a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed        professionally
- Eyes        open and clearly visible
- Full        head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs        or medical reasons
- CHILDREN:        photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness        of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s        head, it is important, that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
The handling time of the various applications will depend on the case load. The handling time is calculated from the moment an application has been registered in the visa handling system.
For Schengen visa, the embassy normally makes a decision in the case within a period of 15 calendar days.  That period may be extended up to a maximum of 30 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed. Exceptionally, when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.

Visa Application Short – Stay Form (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/)
All customers are    required to fill the Visa Application form online on the website: https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/) before    submitting documents at VFS. In case the customers visit Belgium Visa    Application Centre without online application form in advance, they can use    VFS Filling form service with fee applied.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Family Reunion For Father / Mother Of Belgian Minor (Art. 40ter)
The visa for family reunification with Belgian child (ART 40) is given in request for a Long Stay Visa D in order to join child who is a national of Belgium and who lives in Belgium. As a prior requirement, documents submitted must be legalized first.
Customers applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.

## Visa Fees

[bel > vnm > en > Table:Cohabitation with Belgian or non EU citizen > Visa Fees 1]
As of July 1, 2026, long-stay visa fees will amount to 7.750.000 VND. If your appointment takes place after June 30, 2026, you will be required to pay 7.750.000 VND when submitting your Visa D application. 
The applicable visa fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change. The Belgium Embassy in Vietnam is the only entity which is authorized to change the rates provided. All fees are non-refundable unless instructed by the Embassy. 
Visa fee, VFS service fee of VND 682,000 and all optional value added services fees will be charged in cash or card at VFS counter.
For customers’ convenience, VFS provides additional services to support customers in the process of their visa application with VFS. Please “click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/bel/additional-services)”

## Documents Required
Please click here for Documents required

Note: Applicant needs to submit one set of original documents in A4 paper with NO staples, pins.
For more information about legalization procedure, please visit the Embassy’s link: 
https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents (https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents)
All documents to be legalized will be treated directly by the Embassy or the Consulate. The legalized document (if not yet done at the moment of visa submission) will be added to the file later on.

## New contribution for visa LONG stay - D

Since the 2nd of March 2015, a contribution is required for the processing of certain long stay (D) visa applications.
This  contribution should be paid into  the account of the Immigration Office in Belgium.

  The proof  of payment of this contribution needs to be  presented at the  time of the introduction of the visa application. Should  the customer be unable to provide a proof of payment, the visa application  will be deemed inadmissible.

  This contribution is to be paid on top of the handling  fee of the long stay visa (handling fee = the equivalent of €180 in local  currency). The handling fee will still need to be paid at the time of the  application. An exemption for the handling fee, does not necessarily imply that  one is also exempt for the contribution.

  We therefore recommend you to carefully consult the website of the Immigration Office  (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/faq/fee) with regards to the amount of the contribution and the  manner in which this is to be paid.Attention
- Any bank charges or charges       related to the transfer will need to be borne by the customer or whomever       performs the transfer.
- In the event that you have not       paid the full amount, you will have an additional 30 days to transfer the       additional amount. If you do not provide the proof that the full amount       was paid within this period, the       amount already paid will not be reimbursed and the       application will be inadmissible.
- The contribution will       likewise not       be reimbursed in the event of a refusal of the visa.

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards: 
All visa applications must include one recent  photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken        against a plain light background
- Size        35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face        must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral        expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised        eyebrows)
- Eyes        must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair        must not cover face
- No        reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your        eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The        photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have        a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed        professionally
- Eyes        open and clearly visible
- Full        head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs        or medical reasons
- CHILDREN:        photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness        of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s        head, it is important, that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
Processing Time : 15 Calendar Days after application is consider admissible. In individual cases, where further scrutiny of the application is needed by the Immigration department in Brussels, the deadline may be extended up to a maximum of 6 months after the application has been considered admissible.

Visa Application Long-stay Form (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/)
All customers are    required to fill the Visa Application form online on the website: https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/) before    submitting documents at VFS. visa_types.name: Short Stay Visa
visa_types.overview:
If you wish to visit Belgium for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Belgium.
- A visa normally grants you the right to stay in the entire Schengen region. The Schengen countries are: Austria, Belgium, Croatia, the Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, the Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland.
- A visa grants you the right to spend a maximum of 90 days per 6 months in the Schengen region.
- All visa applications should be signed by the customer personally.
- All fields in the application form must be filled in correctly, incomplete forms may cause delay in processing or even rejection.
- The Embassy reserves the right to request additional documents and/or information when processing the application.
For general information about a visa of short stay, you can go on the Web site of The Foreigners Department of the FPS Home Affairs (https://dofi.ibz.be/fr/themes/ressortissants-dun-pays-tiers/court-sejour)(Information available in French, Dutch, and English)
For short stay, you must always submit valid travel insurance on the day you apply for your visa. 
Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The customer is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her  family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance : proof of family bonds with his/her  country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the customer and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This  information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the customer to  leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Family Reunion For Children
In case that your mother or father is Belgian citize or in case that your mother or father to be joined is spouse or partner of Belgian citizen.
customers applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.

## Visa Fees

[bel > vnm > en > Table:Cohabitation with Belgian or non EU citizen > Visa Fees 1]
As of July 1, 2026, long-stay visa fees will amount to 7.750.000 VND. If your appointment takes place after June 30, 2026, you will be required to pay 7.750.000 VND when submitting your Visa D application. 
The applicable visa fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change. The Belgium Embassy in Vietnam is the only entity which is authorized to change the rates provided. All fees are non-refundable unless instructed by the Embassy. 
Visa fee, VFS service fee of VND 682,000 and all optional value added services fees will be charged in cash or card at VFS counter.
For customers’ convenience, VFS provides additional services to support customers in the process of their visa application with VFS. Please “click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/bel/additional-services)”

## Documents Required
Please click here for Documents required

Note: Applicant needs to submit one set of original documents in A4 paper with NO staples, pins.
For more information about legalization procedure, please visit the Embassy’s link: 
https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents (https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents)
All documents to be legalized will be treated directly by the Embassy or the Consulate. The legalized document (if not yet done at the moment of visa submission) will be added to the file later on.

## New contribution for visa LONG stay - D

Since the 2nd of March 2015, a contribution is required for the processing of certain long stay (D) visa applications.

  This contribution can be paid by either the customer or  a third person (family members, acquaintance, sponsor, guarantor, etc.). This  contribution should be paid into  the account of the Immigration Office in Belgium.

  The proof  of payment of this contribution needs to be presented at the time of the introduction of  the visa application. Should the customer be unable to provide a proof of  payment, the visa application will be deemed inadmissible.

  This contribution is to be paid on top of the handling  fee of the long stay visa (handling fee = the equivalent of €180 in local  currency). The handling fee will still need to be paid at the time of the  application. An exemption for the handling fee, does not necessarily imply that  one is also exempt for the contribution.

  We therefore recommend you to carefully consult the website of the Immigration Office  (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/faq/fee) with regards to the amount of the contribution and the  manner in which this is to be paid.Attention
- Any bank charges or charges       related to the transfer will need to be borne by the customer or whomever       performs the transfer.
- In the event that you have not       paid the full amount, you will have an additional 30 days to transfer the       additional amount. If you do not provide the proof that the full amount       was paid within this period,
the       amount already paid will not be reimbursed
and the       application will be inadmissible.
- The  contribution will likewise
not  be reimbursed
in the event of a refusal of the visa.

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards: 
All visa applications must include one recent  photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken        against a plain light background
- Size        35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face        must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral        expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised        eyebrows)
- Eyes        must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair        must not cover face
- No        reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your        eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The        photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have        a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed        professionally
- Eyes        open and clearly visible
- Full        head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs        or medical reasons
- CHILDREN:        photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness        of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s        head, it is important, that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
Processing Time : 15 Calendar Days after application is consider admissible. In individual cases, where further scrutiny of the application is needed by the Immigration department in Brussels, the deadline may be extended up to a maximum of 6 months after the application has been considered admissible.

Visa Application Long-stay Form (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/)
All customers are    required to fill the Visa Application form online on the website: https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/) before    submitting documents at VFS. In case the customers visit Belgium Visa    Application Centre without online application form in advance, they can use    VFS Filling form service with fee applied.

Visit to Family or Friends
customers traveling to Belgium to visit family or friends including girlfriend/boyfriend may apply for this visa.

All documents to be legalized will be treated directly by the Embassy. The legalized document (if not yet done at the moment of visa submission) will be added to the file later on.
Note:
Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The customer is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance: proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the customer and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the customer to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards: 
All visa applications must include one recent  photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken        against a plain light background
- Size        35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face        must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral        expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised        eyebrows)
- Eyes        must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair        must not cover face
- No        reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your        eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The        photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have        a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed        professionally
- Eyes        open and clearly visible
- Full        head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs        or medical reasons
- CHILDREN:        photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness        of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s        head, it is important, that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
The handling time of the various applications will depend on the case load. The handling time is calculated from the moment an application has been registered in the visa handling system.
For Schengen visa, the embassy normally makes a decision in the case within a period of 15 calendar days.  That period may be extended up to a maximum of 30 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed. Exceptionally, when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.

All customers are    required to fill the Visa Application form online on the website: https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be (https://visaonweb.diplomatie.be/) before    submitting documents at VFS. In case the customers visit Belgium Visa    Application Centre without online application form in advance, they can use    VFS Filling form service with fee applied.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Family Reunion
The visa for family reunification with Belgian citizen (ART 40) is given in request for a Long Stay Visa D in order to join husband/wife who is a national of Belgium and who lives in Belgium. As a prior requirement, documents submitted must be legalized first. 
Customers applying for Belgium Long Stay Visa (D Visa) Category are requested to appear in person at the visa application center to register their biometrics which includes scans of all fingerprints and live digital photo.

## Visa Fees

[bel > vnm > en > Table:Cohabitation with Belgian or non EU citizen > Visa Fees 1]
As of July 1, 2026, long-stay visa fees will amount to 7.750.000 VND. If your appointment takes place after June 30, 2026, you will be required to pay 7.750.000 VND when submitting your Visa D application. 
The applicable visa fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change. The Belgium Embassy in Vietnam is the only entity which is authorized to change the rates provided. All fees are non-refundable unless instructed by the Embassy. 
Visa fee, VFS service fee of VND 682,000 and all optional value added services fees will be charged in cash or card at VFS counter.
For customers’ convenience, VFS provides additional services to support customers in the process of their visa application with VFS. Please “click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/bel/additional-services)”

## Documents Required
Please click here for Documents required

Note: Applicant needs to submit one set of original documents in A4 paper with NO staples, pins.
For more information about legalization procedure, please visit the Embassy’s link: 
https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents (https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents)
All documents to be legalized will be treated directly by the Embassy or the Consulate. The legalized document (if not yet done at the moment of visa submission) will be added to the file later on.

## New contribution for visa LONG stay - D

Since the 2nd of March 2015, a contribution is required for the processing of certain long stay (D) visa applications.

  This contribution can be paid by either the customer or  a third person (family members, acquaintance, sponsor, guarantor, etc.). This  contribution should be paid into  the account of the Immigration Office in Belgium.

  The proof  of payment of this contribution needs to be  presented at the  time of the introduction of the visa application. Should  the customer be unable to provide a proof of payment, the visa application  will be deemed inadmissible.

  This contribution is to be paid on top of the handling  fee of the long stay visa (handling fee = the equivalent of €180 in local  currency). The handling fee will still need to be paid at the time of the  application. An exemption for the handling fee, does not necessarily imply that  one is also exempt for the contribution.

   We therefore recommend you to carefully consult the website of the Immigration Office  (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/faq/fee) with regards to the amount of the contribution and the  manner in which this is to be paid.
Attention
- Any bank charges or charges       related to the transfer will need to be borne by the customer or whomever       performs the transfer.
- In the event that you have not       paid the full amount, you will have an additional 30 days to transfer the       additional amount. If you do not provide the proof that the full amount       was paid within this period, the       amount already paid will not be reimbursed and the       application will be inadmissible.
- The contribution will       likewise not       be reimbursed in the event of a refusal of the visa.

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards: 
All visa applications must include one recent  photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken        against a plain light background
- Size        35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face        must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral        expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised        eyebrows)
- Eyes        must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair        must not cover face
- No        reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your        eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The        photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have        a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed        professionally
- Eyes        open and clearly visible
- Full        head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs        or medical reasons
- CHILDREN:        photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness        of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s        head, it is important, that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
Processing Time : 15 Calendar Days after application is consider admissible. In individual cases, where further scrutiny of the application is needed by the Immigration department in Brussels, the deadline may be extended up to a maximum of 6 months after the application has been considered admissible.

In case the customers visit Belgium Visa    Application Centre without online application form in advance, they can use    VFS Filling form service with fee applied.

Overview
Family  Reunion
The  visa for Family  reunion with EU (Non Belgian) Citizen is given in request for a short stay  Visa C in order to join husband/wife or father/mother who is a national of a  country of the European Union (Non Belgian) Citizen and who lives in Belgium. As a prior  requirement, documents submitted must be legalized first.        
Note
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Overview
Cohabitation
The cohabitation in Belgium allows that you can apply for a cohabitation visa and residence permit as an immigrant to Belgium.        
Note
In case you have already submitted your fingerprints for a previous Schengen visa application in the last 59 months, you are not required to submit them again.
However, If you are uncertain whether your fingerprints have been captured for a Schengen visa within the last 59 months, you are advised to appear in person.
Kindly note that, if it would appear that the fingerprints prove not to be present in the VIS system, you will be asked to reappear in person for new biometric data collection. To avoid this possible complication, you have the right to ask for your fingerprints to be taken again.

## Visa Fees

[bel > vnm > en > Table:Tourist Visa > Visa Fees 1]

The applicable visa fee is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change. The Belgium Embassy in Vietnam is the only entity which is authorized to change the rates provided. All fees are non-refundable unless instructed by the Embassy. 
Visa fee, VFS service fee of VND 682,000 and all optional value added services fees will be charged in cash or card at VFS counter.
For customers’ convenience, VFS provides additional services to support customers in the process of their visa application with VFS. Please “click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/bel/additional-services)”

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/vnm/en/bel/travel-insurance)

Please click here for Documents required

Note: Applicant needs to submit one set of original documents in A4 paper with NO staples, pins.
For more information about legalization procedure, please visit the Embassy’s link: 
https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents (https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents)
All documents to be legalized will be treated directly by the Embassy. The legalized document (if not yet done at the moment of visa submission) will be added to the file later on.
Note:
Guarantees for return (assessment of migration risk): The customer is requested to provide as much information as possible to document his/her family background, professional and socio-economic status, ownership of land or real estate (for instance: proof of family bonds with his/her country of origin, proof of regular income and stable source of revenues from the customer and/or his/her partner, proof of property, etc.)
This information is essential to correctly judge the intention of the customer to leave the Schengen area before the expiry of his/her visa.

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards: 
All visa applications must include one recent  photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken        against a plain light background
- Size        35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face        must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral        expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised        eyebrows)
- Eyes        must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair        must not cover face
- No        reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your        eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The        photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have        a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed        professionally
- Eyes        open and clearly visible
- Full        head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs        or medical reasons
- CHILDREN:        photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness        of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s        head, it is important, that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
The handling time of the various applications will depend on the case load. The handling time is calculated from the moment an application has been registered in the visa handling system.
For Schengen visa, the embassy normally makes a decision in the case within a period of 15 calendar days.  That period may be extended up to a maximum of 30 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed. Exceptionally, when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar days.

Visa fee, VFS service fee of VND 682,000 and all optional value added services fees will be charged in cash or card at VFS counter.

## Documents Required
Please click here for Documents required

For more information about legalization procedure, please visit the Embassy’s link: 
https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents (https://vietnam.diplomatie.belgium.be/en/legalisation-documents)
All documents to be legalized will be treated directly by the Embassy. The legalized document (if not yet done at the moment of visa submission) will be added to the file later on.

## New contribution for visa LONG stay - D

Since the 2nd of March 2015, a contribution is required for the processing of certain long stay (D) visa applications.

  This contribution can be paid by either the customer or  a third person (family members, acquaintance, sponsor, guarantor, etc.). This  contribution should be paid into  the account of the Immigration Office in Belgium.
  
  The proof  of payment of this contribution needs to be  presented at the  time of the introduction of the visa application. Should  the customer be unable to provide a proof of payment, the visa application  will be deemed inadmissible.
  
  This contribution is to be paid on top of the handling  fee of the long stay visa (handling fee = the equivalent of €180 in local  currency). The handling fee will still need to be paid at the time of the  application. An exemption for the handling fee, does not necessarily imply that  one is also exempt for the contribution.
  
  We therefore recommend you to carefully consult the website of the Immigration Office  (https://dofi.ibz.be/en/themes/faq/fee) with regards to the amount of the contribution and the  manner in which this is to be paid.
Attention
- Any bank charges or charges related to the transfer will need to be borne by the customer or whomever performs the transfer.
- In the event that you have not paid the full amount, you will have an additional 30 days to transfer the additional amount. If you do not provide the proof that the full amount was paid within this period, the amount already paid will not be reimbursed and the       application will be inadmissible.
- The contribution will likewise not be reimbursed in the event of a refusal of the visa.

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Standards: 
All visa applications must include one recent  photograph in color meeting the compulsory internationally agreed standards.
The basic rules are simple:
- The photo must not be older than 6 months
- Taken        against a plain light background
- Size        35mm by 45mm (IMPORTANT: Passport photos in U.S. format are not accepted)
- Face        must cover 2/3 of photo and looking straight forward
- Neutral        expression please and mouth must be closed (no obvious grinning or raised        eyebrows)
- Eyes        must be on the middle line or in upper half of the photograph
- Hair        must not cover face
- No        reflection or glare on your glasses and the frames should not cover your        eyes (you may find it easier to remove your glasses)
- The        photograph must be in sharp focus and clear
- Have        a strong definition between the face and background
- Printed        professionally
- Eyes        open and clearly visible
- Full        head, without any head covering unless it is worn for religious beliefs        or medical reasons
- CHILDREN:        photographs of children must show a clear image that is a true likeness        of the child. The eyes should be open and if you need to support a baby’s        head, it is important, that your hand cannot be seen in the photograph.

## Processing Time
Processing Time : 15 Calendar Days after application is consider admissible. In individual cases, where further scrutiny of the application is needed by the Immigration department in Brussels, the deadline may be extended up to a maximum of 6 months after the application has been considered admissible.
